name: Synthetic Response Quality Evaluation Search Pass

search_queries.result_count: screened first result page for each targeted query
screened first result page for each targeted query

search_queries.query: "LLM" proxy students real student responses IRT
search_queries.result_count: screened first result page for each targeted query

search_queries.query: "synthetic student response generation" realism diversity evaluation
search_queries.result_count: screened first result page for each targeted query

inclusion_criteria: The source evaluates the quality, realism, fidelity, alignment, or downstream utility of synthetic or simulated student/learner responses.

The source reports quality evidence such as human ratings, psychometric alignment, real-response comparison, embedding/statistical metrics, profile consistency, dialogue metrics, or downstream model utility.
exclusion_criteria: Exclude sources that only describe generation without a recoverable quality-evaluation method.
Exclude chatbot-feedback evaluations when the synthetic student response itself is not evaluated.
dedupe_method: Deduplicated by title, DOI, arXiv identifier, ACL Anthology ID, and canonical source page.

screening_counts.records_identified: at least 30 web results across targeted queries

screening_decisions.title: Leveraging Knowledge Profiles and Generative AI for Realistic Student Response Generation
screening_decisions.reason: Evaluates generated responses with diversity, realism, embedding/PCA comparisons, and training utility against authentic responses.

screening_decisions.title: Agent4Edu: Generating Learner Response Data by Generative Agents for Intelligent Education Systems
screening_decisions.reason: Evaluates generated learner responses against human learner responses with consistency and discrepancy metrics.

screening_decisions.title: Calibrating Generative AI to Produce Realistic Essays for Data Augmentation
screening_decisions.reason: Evaluates simulated essays with expert scoring, agreement metrics, score-distribution comparisons, and realism ratings.

screening_decisions.title: Generative Students: Using LLM-Simulated Student Profiles to Support Question Item Evaluation
screening_decisions.reason: Evaluates simulated student MCQ responses through profile alignment, real-student correlation, and overlap in difficult items.

screening_decisions.title: Can LLMs Reliably Simulate Real Students' Abilities in Mathematics and Reading Comprehension?
screening_decisions.reason: Evaluates LLM proxy-student alignment by mapping 11 model responses onto the same IRT ability scale as real NAEP populations.

screening_decisions.title: Simulated Students in Tutoring Dialogues: Substance or Illusion?
screening_decisions.reason: Defines and benchmarks simulated-student quality metrics spanning linguistic, behavioral, and cognitive aspects with automated and human evaluation.

screening_decisions.title: A Framework for Designing an AI Chatbot to Support Scientific Argumentation
screening_decisions.reason: The paper uses synthetic student inputs to evaluate chatbot feedback quality, but it does not primarily evaluate the quality or realism of the synthetic student inputs themselves.

screening_summary: Targeted bootstrap screening included six sources covering embedding/statistical realism checks, downstream training utility, learner-response consistency metrics, expert essay realism and score preservation, profile-alignment comparisons to real MCQ responses, IRT ability-scale alignment with real NAEP populations, and dialogue-simulation metrics spanning linguistic, behavioral, and cognitive fidelity.
